channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: High Court grants interim injunction protecting journalist in defamation case, limits further statements
item.description: A Kenyan High Court has issued a temporary injunction protecting Nation Media Group journalist Simon Ciuri in an ongoing defamation case against directors of Willstone Homes Limited. The dispute arose...

item.title: Inside Sydney’s long-running private pub lunch club of industry figures
item.description: An exclusive and long-running private dining group made up of senior figures from Sydney’s hospitality and pub industry has continued its monthly tradition of boozy lunches for more than half a centur...

item.title: Eight Portable Power Banks for Staying Charged During Long Travel Days
item.description: The article highlights a selection of eight portable chargers designed to keep travelers’ devices powered during long and often unpredictable travel days, particularly in airports and during delays. I...

item.title: Surreal winter sunrise clouds captivate Cape Town’s Helderberg and Strand regions
item.description: Early morning skies over the Helderberg region and Strand in Cape Town delivered a striking natural display as unusual cloud formations combined with vivid winter sunrise colours. Along the R44 near t...

item.title: Switzerland defeat Algeria 2–0 to book place in World Cup Round of 16
item.description: Switzerland produced a disciplined and tactical performance to beat Algeria 2–0 and secure their place in the World Cup last 16. The match, played at BC Place in Vancouver, saw Switzerland striker Bre...

item.title: England back Henry Pollock ahead of Springboks Test at Ellis Park
item.description: England head coach Steve Borthwick has expressed strong confidence in young loose forward Henry Pollock ahead of their Test match against the Springboks at Ellis Park on Saturday. Pollock, aged 21, ha...

item.title: Job Creation Slows as U.S. Labor Report Shows Mixed Economic Signals
item.description: The Department of Labor's latest economic report reveals concerning trends despite a declining unemployment rate of 4.2%. Job creation fell sharply to 57,000 over three months, down from 129,000 in th...

item.title: How Sudanese Music Influenced the 2019 Revolution Against Bashir
item.description: The article explains how music played a very central role in Sudan’s 2019 revolution that led to the removal of Omar al-Bashir. Far from being just background entertainment, music in Sudan became a po...

item.title: 100-day real-world test reveals persistent limitations in self-driving car systems on Australian roads
item.description: Researchers conducted an extensive real-world study using a Tesla Model Y equipped with Full Self-Driving (FSD) software over more than 100 days on Queensland roads in Australia. Across this period, t...

item.title: IMD issues orange alert in Mumbai as heavy rains continue; monsoon reaches Delhi after delay
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for Mumbai, Thane, Palghar, and Raigad, warning of moderate to intense rainfall at isolated locations. Mumbai has been experiencing...

item.title: Kenya Government Announces Preparedness Measures Ahead of Expected El Niño Rains
item.description: The Kenyan government has announced that it is fully prepared to handle the anticipated El Niño rains expected in the country, assuring citizens that there is no need for panic. Deputy President Profe...

item.title: Key African developments: Ebola trial begins in DR Congo, diplomatic tensions, and ICC withdrawal moves
item.description: This edition of Africa-wide news on 3 July 2026 highlights several major political, health, and legal developments across the continent. In the Democratic Republic of Congo, a clinical trial has offic...

item.title: Investigation finds lamb doner kebabs contained mostly non-sheep meat in major UK food fraud case
item.description: An investigation by BBC News has uncovered a major food fraud case involving Kismet Kebabs, one of the UK's largest doner kebab suppliers. DNA testing carried out as part of trading standards checks r...

item.title: House prosecution urges Sara Duterte to attend impeachment trial proceedings
item.description: A House impeachment prosecutor has expressed hope that Vice President Sara Duterte will personally appear in her impeachment trial, underscoring the importance of participation in the legal and consti...

item.title: Madison Square Garden reportedly hosts high-profile wedding preparations for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce
item.description: Madison Square Garden in New York City is reportedly preparing to host what is expected to be a highly publicised wedding between pop star Taylor Swift and NFL player Travis Kelce. According to detail...

item.title: Lorenzo Lamas’ divorce expected to be finalized in November 2026 amid relationship with Heather Locklear
item.description: Actor Lorenzo Lamas is expected to become legally single by November 2026 following a court-approved move to expedite the finalization of his divorce from Kenna Nicole Lamas. A Los Angeles Superior Co...

item.title: Extreme Weather Patterns in Canada This Summer
item.description: Canada is experiencing extreme weather events across various regions this summer, including intense storms in Western Canada causing severe flooding, heat waves and thunderstorms in Ontario, Quebec, a...

item.title: Rivers State Government Moves to Resolve Bille Gas Leakage and Upgrade Local Health Facility
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Sir Siminalayi Fubara, don call for quick and full resolution of the gas leakage wey happen for Bille community inside Degema Local Government Area of the state. The governor al...

item.description: The live coverage reports on ongoing Round of 32 matches at the FIFA World Cup 2026, highlighting key developments across several fixtures. In Vancouver, Switzerland delivered a strong performance aga...

item.title: Severe June heatwave in Western Europe linked to 3,700 excess deaths across France, Belgium, and the Netherlands
item.description: A severe heatwave that affected parts of Western Europe in late June 2026 has been linked to at least 3,700 excess deaths across France, Belgium, and the Netherlands, according to preliminary governme...
